commands.repair_reason: Needs live behavior proof and maintainer review of the session-state risk rather than an automated code repair; I found no discrete patch defect to hand to autofix.; Cleared: The diff only changes TypeScript session-lock logic and adds a focused test; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, package resolution, or other supply-chain-sensitive surfaces. (sha=63a66f5f3c1c43ba2bbfb867120cad95c5433c46)
commands.review_summary:
The PR tracks reacquired embedded-run session write locks, abandons still-held in-flight locks during attempt cleanup, and adds a focused regression test for a stuck auto-compaction lock.

Reproducibility: yes. with medium confidence from source plus the linked issue logs: current main can hold a reacquired session write lock until a never-settling compaction wrapper returns, while later turns hit the 60000ms acquisition timeout. I did not run the live Discord/Opus reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs live behavior proof and maintainer review of the session-state risk rather than an automated code repair; I found no discrete patch defect to hand to autofix.

target.title: fix(agents): abandon hung in-flight write lock on attempt cleanup (#84193)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84193 — post-run Pi auto-compaction can hang while holding the session JSONL write lock. After the run's outer abort/timeout fires the wrapped `await run()` body never settles, so the lock-controller's `finally` never runs, so the lock keeps living in-process for the entire `maxHoldMs` window (defaults to runTimeout + 900s compaction grace = many minutes). Every later turn on the same Discord channel then bounces off `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError: session file locked (timeout 60000ms)` until a Gateway restart.

This change tracks every lock reacquired through `withSessionWriteLock` and abandons the still-held entries from `acquireForCleanup`, so the next turn can acquire the session file immediately. The existing transcript fence still rejects later writes that would have raced a partial-compaction mutation.

## What changed

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`
  - Wrap each reacquired write lock in a small `InFlightLockEntry` and add it to a per-controller `Set`.
  - New `abandonInFlightWriteLocks()` flips the controller into takeover state and force-releases every still-held entry (idempotent via the entry's `released` flag).
  - `acquireForCleanup()` calls it before re-acquiring, so a hung compact() can never keep the JSONL lock alive past attempt teardown. Also releases the lingering `heldLock` (initial coarse lock) if it was still held when takeover was detected.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.compaction-leak.test.ts` (new)
  - Reproduces the leak with a stuck `withSessionWriteLock(() => never-resolve)` and proves cleanup releases it.
  - Asserts further writes after the abandon are rejected via the existing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` fence path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: post-run Pi auto-compaction holds the session JSONL write lock past the run's outer timeout, blocking every later turn for the same session with `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` until Gateway restart. (#84193)

Real environment tested: macOS arm64, Node v25.9.0, vitest 4.1.6 from `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` in this branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.compaction-leak.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts \
  src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```
 Test Files  8 passed (8)
      Tests  112 passed (112)
   Duration  628ms
```

Same regression suite **on `upstream/main` without this patch** fails at:

```
embedded attempt session lock — stuck auto-compaction (#84193)
  ✗ abandons in-flight write lock when cleanup runs while a hung compact() still owns it
    expected "vi.fn()" to be called 1 times, but got 0 times
  ✗ rejects further withSessionWriteLock calls after abandoning a hung in-flight lock
    expected [Function] to throw error matching /session file changed/ but got
    'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'release')'
```

(reproduced by `git stash push src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts` and re-running the same `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` command — the leak path was confirmed to flip from red to green by this patch alone.)

Observed result after fix: cleanup releases the stuck reacquired lock immediately. The 60s acquire timeout no longer blocks the next turn. Transcript fencing still rejects further writes after abandon via the existing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`.

What was not tested: live Discord + Anthropic Opus session with real Pi auto-compaction stall. Tests cover the lock-controller surface only; the existing higher-level `attempt.ts` cleanup already wires `acquireForCleanup` into the `finally` block (`attempt.ts:4832`), so the abandon fires automatically on any aborted/timed-out attempt without further changes.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.compaction-leak.test.ts` — 4/4 pass
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts` — 28/28 pass (unchanged)
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts` — pass
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts` — pass
- [x] `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files — 0 errors / 0 warnings
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on touched files — formatted
- [x] Live Discord/Opus auto-compaction reproduction (left for maintainer / Crabbox)
target.author: yetval
